internal_forecasts.label: Cloud Revenue FY2025
internal_forecasts.note: Board-approved base case at $23.1B. Public commitment set at the stretch target. Internal FP&A flagged the $25B figure as achievable only with full OCI capacity delivery and no macro headwinds.

internal_forecasts.label: Cloud Revenue FY2025
internal_forecasts.note: Updated model after strong Q1 FY25 OCI results. Internal base case raised to $24.0B. Gap to public commitment narrowing but not closed. Capex delivery still the binding constraint on upside.

internal_forecasts.label: Cloud Revenue FY2025
internal_forecasts.note: Internal model at $24.6B heading into H2 FY25. Aggressive Q4 new bookings and OCI capacity additions gave management confidence to maintain the public $25B floor.

internal_forecasts.label: OCI YoY Growth Rate (FY2025 H2)
internal_forecasts.note: Internal ops plan modeled 44% OCI growth for the back half of FY25. Larry Ellison cited 52% on the earnings call, which included several large multi-year sovereign AI contracts not yet in the internal revenue model.

internal_forecasts.label: Cloud Revenue FY2026
internal_forecasts.note: FY2026 target set publicly at $48B at the FY25 Q4 earnings call. Internal FP&A base case was $44B, with $48B treated as the board-level aspiration. Largest single gap in the dataset — $4B of public commitment ahead of internal plan.

internal_forecasts.label: Cloud Revenue FY2026
internal_forecasts.note: After OCI growth accelerated to 56% in Q1 FY26, internal model was revised sharply upward to $49B. Public guidance raised to $50B — now closely tracking internal view. Governance gap closed to $1B.

internal_forecasts.label: Cloud Revenue FY2026
internal_forecasts.note: Internal model now exceeds the public $50B guidance as OCI growth continues to beat plan. Public number is serving as a conservative floor — a reversal of the earlier pattern where public led internal. Consistent with disciplined upside management ahead of formal guidance raise.

internal_forecasts.label: Cloud Revenue FY2027
internal_forecasts.note: Larry Ellison announced $65B FY2027 target on the call. Internal long-range plan shows $58B base case and $65B as the upside scenario requiring full capex delivery and no competitive share loss. This is the widest gap between internal and external on a long-range commitment — consistent with prior pattern of aspirational public targets on multi-year horizons.
